feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Community
feed.subtitle: SAP Community blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Hello SAP community! My name is Shlok Shigli and I'm excited to start my journey
entry.content:
My name is Shlok Shigli, and I am currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I’m excited to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem, learn from experienced professionals, and connect with people across the global SAP network.

As a student, I am passionate about learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage operations more efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I look forward to gaining insights from experts, discovering new ideas, and building meaningful professional connections.

I’m excited to share my learning journey and contribute to the SAP Community as I continue to grow.

Thank you, SAP Community, for this wonderful opportunity!

Here are the questions I've chosen to answer as a part of the Hello SAP Community initiative:

1. What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to build a successful career at the intersection of technology and engineering. I want to continuously learn new skills and work on innovative solutions that help organizations improve efficiency and make better decisions. I also hope to contribute to the tech community and grow as a professional in the global technology ecosystem.

2. Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next five years, I see myself as a skilled technology professional with strong technical knowledge and hands-on experience. I hope to work on real-world projects, continuously improve my expertise, and contribute positively to my organization and the wider technology community.

3. What is your hobby?

One of my hobbies is exploring new technologies and learning about innovations in engineering and business systems. I enjoy reading, learning new skills online, and continuously expanding my knowledge through different learning platforms.

4. Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

  • Build a strong career in the technology and engineering field
  • Continuously improve myself by learning new technologies and skills
  • Work on projects that create real-world impact
  • Build meaningful connections and collaborate with talented people
  • Maintain a balanced life while pursuing personal and professional growth

5. What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

One of the most important lessons my parents taught me is to stay disciplined and never stop learning. They always reminded me that progress takes time and consistency. Their guidance has helped me stay focused on my goals and approach challenges with patience and determination.

My Experience at SAP Inside Track Bengaluru

SAP Inside Track Bengaluru 2026 – Excited to be part of this amazing SAP Community event

 Recently, I had the opportunity to attend SAP Inside Track Bengaluru, and it was an incredibly valuable learning experience for me as a student exploring the SAP ecosystem.

SAP campus in Bengaluru
Being part of an event where SAP professionals and community members shared their knowledge was very inspiring. It helped me better understand how SAP technologies are used to solve real-world business problems and improve organizational efficiency.

One of the most interesting parts of the event was interacting with SAP experts and fellow learners. Listening to their experiences and insights gave me a broader perspective on the different opportunities within the SAP ecosystem.

Key Takeaways from the Event

  • The growing importance of AI and intelligent technologies in enterprise solutions
  • How SAP platforms help organizations improve efficiency and business processes
  • The strong role of the SAP Community in sharing knowledge and supporting learners.

An insightful session on Generative AI and AI Agents,

I’m excited to continue learning and sharing my journey with the SAP Community as I explore more about SAP technologies.

And now let’s hear from you! 

I would love to hear your thoughts as well.

Which technology excites you the most right now, and why? 

I’d love to hear your thoughts as well. Feel free to share your ideas and predictions in the comments below. I’m looking forward to reading your perspectives and hearing what you think about the ideas I’ve shared.

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Dino C D and I'm a Cybersecurity Graduate with a Passion for IoT, AI
entry.content:
Speaking about my patent-pending IoT Device at an competition, Bengaluru.

 

 

Hello SAP Community!

My name is Dino C D and I'm a postgraduate student pursuing my MSc in Artificial Intelligence & Cybersecurity at Christ University, BYC, Bengaluru. I completed my Bachelor of Computer Applications at St Joseph's University, Bengaluru, where I built a strong foundation in programming, cloud deployment, full-stack development, and IoT engineering.

Beyond academics, I'm a multi-award-winning innovator (3× 1st Prize for my IoT IV Blood Detector Device), a patent applicant, an active cybersecurity community contributor through S.H.I.E.L.D, and a researcher working on audio deepfake detection. I love building things that solve real problems — whether that's a smart medical device, an interactive history website, or a security-aware web application.

Pitching my patent-pending IoT IV Blood Detector to a C-DAC judge

 

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a leading cybersecurity engineer who doesn't just defend systems — but actively shapes the way security is built into technology from the ground up. I want to work at the intersection of AI and cybersecurity, developing intelligent threat detection systems that can anticipate attacks before they happen.

On a bigger scale, I want to contribute to making healthcare technology safer. My IoT IV Blood Detector project opened my eyes to how vulnerable critical medical infrastructure can be. My dream is to see a world where hospital systems, patient data, and medical IoT devices are protected by design — and I want to be part of building that future. A patent in progress feels like the first small step toward that dream.

 

 

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I see myself working as a SOC Analyst or Cybersecurity Researcher at a technology or cybersecurity firm — ideally contributing to threat intelligence, incident response, or AI-powered security tooling. I hope to have completed my MSc, earned certifications like CompTIA Security+ and CEH, and published at least one peer-reviewed research paper (starting with my current work on audio deepfake detection).

I also see myself mentoring the next wave of cybersecurity students — much like the mentors and communities that shaped me. If the journey goes the way I hope, perhaps I'll be sharing a very different blog post on the SAP Community in 2031, looking back at how it all started. 😊

Why did you choose MSc Artificial Intelligence & Cybersecurity at Christ University?

Honestly, it felt like the perfect convergence of everything I was already passionate about. After my BCA, I knew I wanted to go deeper — not just into cybersecurity, but into how AI is reshaping the entire threat landscape. Christ University's programme offered exactly that combination.

What truly won me over was the learning environment. The workshops have been eye-opening — hands-on, practical, and constantly pushing you to think beyond textbooks. The classes don't just teach concepts, they challenge you to question, build, and apply. Every session feels like it's preparing you for a problem that actually exists in the real world.

I'm genuinely grateful for the exposure this course has given me — from AI-driven security techniques to research opportunities. It's not just a degree; it's the foundation I needed to become the cybersecurity professional I want to be. 🎓


What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

Without a doubt: Temporal Explorer — Past to Future.

The idea was simple but ambitious — build an interactive website that lets users travel through time, from ancient civilisations all the way to speculative futures. We used HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to create animated timelines, artefact showcases, and immersive storytelling experiences.

What made it so fun wasn't just the tech — it was the creativity involved. We debated what the future might look like, researched historical artefacts, and designed transitions that felt like you were genuinely moving through eras. Completing it in time for the Web Weavers intercollegiate competition and walking away with 2nd place made it even more rewarding. It reminded me that the best projects aren't just technically sound — they tell a story.

What is your hobby?

My biggest hobby is community work and volunteering. Over the past few years, I've coordinated 50+ outreach events with AICUF, taught constitutional literacy in government schools through KJES, and helped build the cybersecurity awareness culture at Christ University through S.H.I.E.L.D.

It might sound unusual as a "hobby," but there's something deeply energising about connecting with people, solving real-world problems together, and seeing the direct impact of your effort — whether that's a student who finally understands their civic rights, or a community that feels a little more supported. It keeps me grounded in why I'm pursuing technology in the first place: to serve people.

A volunteering Social work which was held in Biccodu, Karnataka by KJES


What do you imagine or predict is the future of your topic — AI & Cybersecurity?

The future is fascinating — and a little terrifying, honestly. Here's my prediction:

AI will become the most powerful weapon in both attack and defence simultaneously. We're already seeing AI-generated phishing emails that are indistinguishable from real ones, deepfake audio used in fraud (which is exactly why I'm researching it), and autonomous malware that adapts in real time. The adversarial AI race is already here.

On the defence side, I believe AI-powered SOC platforms will eventually handle 90% of threat triage autonomously — flagging only the most complex incidents for human analysts. Security will shift from "detect and respond" to "predict and prevent."

The wildcard? IoT security. Billions of connected devices — many with zero security baked in — will be the biggest attack surface of the next decade. My IV detector project taught me first-hand how exposed medical IoT can be. I believe the next generation of cybersecurity professionals will spend as much time securing physical devices as they do securing data.

The future belongs to those who understand both the intelligence of AI and the vulnerabilities it creates.

Share a fun fact about yourself that people don't know!

I once helped coordinate a community event managing ₹2.5 lakh in organisational expenditure — and I was a second-year undergrad student at the time. 

As the core committee member for CHRONICLES (AICUF's major fest at St Joseph's University), I was suddenly responsible for budgeting, resource allocation, vendor coordination, and keeping everything on track. No one tells you that community leadership is basically a crash course in project management, crisis handling, and people skills — all at once.

Oh, and I did all of that while simultaneously building AWS deployments, learning Kali Linux, and developing IoT devices in my spare time. Sleep was optional, apparently.

With Justice Kurian Joseph, Retired Judge of the Supreme Court of India — Chief Guest at CHRONICLES, St Joseph's University, Bengaluru. ⚖️

 

And now let's hear from you!

What do you imagine or predict is the future of AI and Cybersecurity — and how are you preparing for it? Drop your thoughts in the comments below — I'd love to hear where you think the field is heading! 🚀

Dino C D | MSc AI & Cybersecurity Student, Christ University, Bengaluru
🔗linkedin.com/in/dinocd | 💻 github.com/Dino20004

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! I'm Nikith Nagbushan Urs - A Student Developer
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Nikith Nagbushan Urs, and I’m currently a third-year B.E. student in Computer Science Engineering (Data Science) at New Horizon College of Engineering, Bengaluru.

Alongside my academic journey, I currently serve as the President of the Innovation Club, where I work closely with teams of students to build innovative projects, organize technical events, and encourage creative problem-solving within the campus.

My interests lie in application development, innovation, and building platforms that make systems more efficient and accessible. One of the projects I worked on was the Central NHCE Application, which aims to centralize campus information and improve communication between faculty and students.

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as part of the "Hello SAP community"  initiative.

What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

One of the most exciting projects I worked on was developing the Central NHCE Application for my campus.

The goal of the application was to create a centralized platform where students can easily access important campus information, announcements, and updates. One of the major features of the application is department specific announcements, which helps bridge the communication gap between faculty and students.

The application also focuses on a clean and user-friendly interface, allowing students to quickly navigate and find relevant information.

Working on this project was extremely rewarding because it allowed me to build something that can directly benefit students across the campus while making communication more structured and professional.

What do you imagine or predict is the future of AI?

I believe that AI will become one of the most powerful tools humanity can use.

While AI may automate many processes and make tasks easier, I strongly believe that core human emotions, creativity, and decision-making will always remain uniquely human.

AI should not be viewed as a threat but rather as a powerful tool and companion that enhances human capabilities. For people who learn how to work with AI and understand its potential, it will open many new opportunities.

In the future, I believe the world will move toward collaboration between humans and AI, where technology helps us solve problems faster and more efficiently.

What is your hobby?

One of my favorite hobbies is bike riding.

Bike rides give me the opportunity to relax and clear my mind after long hours of studying or working on projects. Riding allows me to explore new places, enjoy the journey, and take a break from screens and technology.

Sometimes, the best ideas for projects and innovations come to me while I’m out on a ride.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

One of the most important lessons my parents have taught me is to always respect elders and treat everyone with kindness.

No matter what achievements we accomplish in life, values such as respect, humility, and kindness define our character. These principles guide the way I interact with people in both my personal life and professional journey.

And now let’s hear from you!

What do you think will be the biggest impact of AI on the future of technology and society?

I would love to hear your thoughts in the comments!

entry.title: Hello SAP community! My name is Mohammed Asif and i'm excited to start my journey.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community

My name is Mohammed Asif, and I’m currently pursuing Mechanical Engineering at REVA University in Bangalore, India.

I am very happy to be part of the Hello SAP Community initiative. This opportunity allows beginners like me to explore the SAP ecosystem and connect with professionals around the world.

As a student, I am interested in learning new technologies and understanding how SAP solutions help businesses manage their operations efficiently. Through the SAP Community, I hope to learn from experts, discover new ideas, and grow my professional network.

I look forward to sharing my learning journey and contributing to the  SAP  Community in the future.

Thank you SAP Community for this wonderful opportunity!

Here are the questions I chose to answer as a part of the initiative:

1.what is your professional life dream ?

My professional dream is to build a successful career in technology and engineering. I want to continuously learn new skills and work on innovative solutions that help organizations improve their efficiency. I also aim to contribute to the tech community and grow as a professional in the global technology ecosystem.

2.where do you see yourself in 5 years ?

In the next five years, I see myself as a skilled professional working in the technology industry. I hope to gain strong knowledge, practical experience, and work on real-world projects. I also want to keep learning and improving myself while contributing positively to my organization and community.

3.what is your hobby ?

One of my hobbies is learning about new technologies and exploring new ideas related to engineering and innovation. I also enjoy reading, learning new skills online, and improving my knowledge through different educational platforms.

4. which 4 thing do you absolutely want to achives in life ?

There are several goals I want to achieve in my life:

  • Build a successful professional career.
  • Continuously learn and develop new skills.
  • Contribute to society through knowledge and innovation.
  • Inspire others through my journey and experiences.

5.what is the best lesson your parents taught you ?

The best lesson my parents taught me is the importance of hard work, honesty, and patience. They always encouraged me to stay focused on my goals and never give up during difficult situations. These values continue to guide me in my education and future career.

 

My SAP TechEd Bangalore 2025 

 

 

My Experience at SAP TechEd Bangalore

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore, and it was an amazing learning experience for me. As a student interested in technology and innovation, being part of such a global tech event was truly inspiring. One of the most exciting parts of the event was exploring the Demo Pods, where SAP experts explained different technologies and solutions. It was great to see how SAP technologies like BTP are used to solve real-world business problems.

Key Takeaways & Highlights
While there was a lot to take in, these three themes defined the event for me:

The Power of Generative AI: The evolution of SAP Joule was front and center. It’s clear that AI is no longer a "plus-one" feature but the core engine driving developer productivity and business process automation.

A "Clean Core" is Non-Negotiable: There was a significant focus on the Clean Core Strategy. Learning about new extensibility patterns reinforced that staying close to the standard is the only way to ensure long-term agility and easier upgrades.

Driving Sustainability with Data: I was particularly inspired by the sessions on ESG reporting. Seeing how we can leverage BTP to turn sustainability goals into measurable data points is a game-changer for the modern enterprise.

 

 

 

That's a great photo! Capturing moments with a  community members like that adds a lot of authenticity

The Best Part of SAP TechEd:

The Community  While the product announcements and technical roadmaps are what bring us to SAP TechEd Bangalore, it’s the people who make the experience unforgettable. One of my personal highlights was the chance to connect directly with fellow community members and SAP experts on the show floor. Whether we were discussing the nuances of BTP at the Demo Pods or simply sharing our learning journeys, these interactions are where the real "tech magic" happens.

SAP TechEd Bangalore – A Great Learning Experience with My College Team:


I had the wonderful opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore along with friends from my college. It was a great experience for all of us to be part of such a global technology event. Our college group explored different sessions, demo pods, and interacted with SAP experts. We also had the chance to connect with members of the SAP Community and learn about new technologies like SAP BTP, AI, and cloud innovations.

Now, I would like to finish the post by asking you all a question which you're invited to answer in the comments section:

Which SAP modules offer the best career growth for beginners?

 

 

 

 

 

#HelloSAPcommunity #SAPcommunity #SAPLearning #SAPForBeginners #TechCommunity #FutureWithSAP

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Adithya Ajith and I’m excited to begin my journey here
entry.content:
My name is Adithya Ajith and I’m a student at Christ University , Bengaluru , India.

I am currently in the third semester of my MSc in Data Science, and I completed my bachelor’s in Computer Applications. I enjoy exploring the intricacies of data and working with datasets by applying different models to identify meaningful insights and outcomes.

I am delighted to be a part of the Hello SAP Community initiative and look forward to learning, sharing ideas, and connecting with people in the SAP ecosystem.

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as part of the initiative.

What are my hobbies? 

One of my favorite hobbies is reading, especially psychological thrillers with the suspenseful plots and complex characters. I also enjoy emotionally powerful stories, particularly the works of Khaled Hosseini . His writing captures human emotions and relationships.

Roaming around college with a book in hand

My reading list is quite long, and I’m currently looking forward to reading When the Lemon Trees Grow and The Palace of Illusions. If you’ve read either of these, I’d love to hear your thoughts!

From a recent trek

 

Along with reading, I enjoy exploring new places and hope to travel more in the coming years as part of my bucket list. I  like watching Urdu dramas for the poetic beauty of the language. I also enjoy learning new languages and regularly use Duolingo to practice German.

What is the best lesson my parents taught me? 

My parents taught me to respect every individual who crosses my path, regardless of their designation, age, gender, or any other external or material characteristics. 

This  has helped shape the way I view the world and further encouraged me to understand people beyond surface-level differences , I believe I have grown into a more considerate and empathetic person, both in my personal life and in my interactions with others.

What is my personal life dream?  

My biggest personal dream is to take my parents on trips across the country and around the world, especially my mother, who truly loves to travel. Being able to give them those experiences would mean a lot to me, as they have always supported me throughout my journey.

Another dream close to my heart is to provide meals once every month, to an old age home and an orphanage ,meals that they may not normally have access to and that are often considered a luxury. 

What was the most fun project I ever participated in and why?    

One of the most meaningful and enjoyable projects I participated in was during my bachelor’s when I was heading the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) initiative. The project focused on making simple beds for people living on roadsides, especially for children who often sleep on hard surfaces.

As part of the project, we visited different cloth manufacturing companies to collect fabric scraps and waste cloth. The plan was to connect and braid these pieces together to create durable beds. The initiative involved a lot of teamwork, thoughtful discussions, and brainstorming sessions, making it a very memorable experience.

What do I imagine or predict is the future of AI? 

No Traffic Signals, AI Footwear, and No More Manual Home Cleaning !

I predict a future where AI becomes a natural part of our everyday lives. Vehicles fully guided by AI, using satellite data and sensors to detect other vehicles and automatically choosing the best routes, making traffic signals unnecessary and reducing congestion. AI integrated footwear, where shoes automatically detect the size and arch of a person’s feet and adjust for the perfect fit. In homes, AI-based dust detection systems with centralized vacuum mechanism  automatically clean dust as soon as it accumulates, eliminating the need for daily sweeping ,wiping and preventing the asthma related problems.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore experience

Attending SAP TechEd @ Bangalore this year, was truly an incredible experience. It felt great to meet senior professionals from SAP and see how humble and approachable they were while interacting with us. I also had the chance to interact with people from different companies, learn about the opportunities available in the SAP ecosystem, and understand how organizations are integrating new technologies into their products and workplaces.

During the event, I also appeared for the Generative AI certification at the center. Even when there were some technical glitches, the team remained extremely calm and supportive, which was very reassuring. Another thing that left a strong impression on me was the confidence and clarity of the speakers throughout the event.

When I met Joule

The event also gave me the opportunity to expand my network, learn directly from industry experts, and gain a clearer perspective on the future of enterprise technology. 

At the SAP TechEd EVent @ Bangalore

I was also introduced to the SAP Community, a platform where people share ideas not only about technology but also about their hobbies, interests, and experiences. It was Moshe Naveh who formally introduced a group of us college students to the community and encouraged us to start contributing. This encouragement motivated me to write my first SAP Community blog, where I shared my reflections on emerging technologies and their impact on society. If you’re interested, feel free to check out my first blog here : Emerging Technology and Caged Thoughts 

Main highlights and SAP TechEd key takeaways 

One of the main highlights of SAP TechEd was learning how SAP is rapidly advancing  to build intelligent enterprise applications. The demo sessions showcased how AI and data are shaping the future of enterprise technology.

Another key takeaway was from the hands-on technical sessions, where we explored tools such as Knowledge Graphs in HANA Cloud, ABAP Cloud, UI5 development, AI agents, Cloud SDK, and MCP servers with CAP applications. The Generative AI deep-dive sessions on prompt optimization, benchmarking, and model evaluation were especially insightful and helped me understand practical approaches to building AI-driven workflows.

Overall, the event highlighted how AI, cloud platforms, and modern data architectures are transforming the way enterprises build and scale applications.

And now let’s hear from you! 😎

I would love to hear your thoughts as well.

What do you imagine or predict is the future of your topic (AI, Cloud, or any technology you are passionate about)?🤔

Share your ideas in the comments below. I’m really excited to read your perspectives and predictions. Also let me know what you think about my ideas!😊

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Moshe Naveh and I'm really excited about the new journey ahead of us
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

As you have probably read in my previous blog post about SAP TechEd 2025 and on Social media, this year’s SAP TechEd was unique and unforgettable in many ways. During the recent SAP TechEd season I had many special SAP Community moments: great conversations, made a lot of new community friends and often I was inspired by the people I met, each person in his or her unique topic and way. All those interactions started with “Hello! I’m Moshe from the SAP Community.” After after coming back from SAP TechEd Bangalore I thought how can we bring the same great energy that sparked during SAP TechEd to our SAP Community, so we can benefit from it year-round.

Today I’m very excited to announce a new SAP Community initiative, “Hello SAP Community”. The “Hello SAP Community” initiative will take us on an incredible journey to get to know the next generation of SAP professionals: what interests them, how they already shine in their career, their vision of the future, how they grow in the era of AI, their hobbies and much more!

When I got to meet SAP's next generation I was impressed by their creativity, energy, ambition and many other fascinating aspects! This sentiment only enhanced after I was fortunate to collaborate with our initiative’s first participants on their blog posts. I’ve learned so much in the process and I look forward to learning more from their future engagement in the SAP Community. This initiative, like the entire the SAP Community, regardless of how experienced you are in the SAP world, will enable us all to keep growing together!. 

So how will “Hello SAP Community” initiative run?

As we are targeting people who are new to the SAP world, we decided to host this initiative in the Beginners’ corner group. Our pilot of the initiative will start by featuring awesome students from Bangalore area . I was very fortunate to meet many of them in SAP TechEd Bangalore. Every week the participants will post introduction blog posts about themselves that will allow us to get to know them, learn from them and connect. They will answer preset questions and every participant will also add their own unique touch, insights and experiences. The SAP Community will gain fresh insights, interesting stories and a unique glimpse into the journey of becoming an SAP professional in the AI era, making it fascinating more than ever! The participants will gain the opportunity to share with the world the great things they are doing, their future plans, get a great advise from experienced SAP professionals and network. 

In order to be updated when new great blog posts are published, please visit the dedicated “Hello SAP Community” user tag and subscribe to it:

If you are new to the SAP world and would like to join the initiative and share about your journey, please send us an Email community@sap.com and we will send you further details about joining the initiative:).

Here are the preset questions that the participants were invited to chose from:

  • What is your professional life dream?  
  • Where do you see yourself in 5 years?  
  • How do you think the way you were raised affects your Career?  
  • What is your hobby?
  • What is a fun fact/story about yourself that people don't know?
  • Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?  
  • What is the best lesson your parents taught you?  
  • If you could be a superhero (or are in your spare time) who would you be? What do you enjoy most in your work/studies and why?  
  • What is your personal life dream?  
  • What do you imagine or predict is the future of your topic? (AI, Cloud etc., be creative be daring, be entertaining)  
  • What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why? 

I invite you all to either answer one of these questions in the comments section or simply say Hello SAP Community:) 

I’m looking forward to another great SAP Community journey that started with a simple hello!:) I already encourage you all to read the amazing posts that were published!:)).

entry.title: SAP — серед ТОП-50 AI-лідерів України! 🚀
entry.content:
SAP увійшла до національного рейтингу ТОП-50 AI-лідерів України від ділового видання DELO.ua у категорії IT, підтверджуючи статус компанії як одного з ключових драйверів впровадження штучного інтелекту в українському бізнесі.​

Рейтинг DELO.ua відзначає компанії, що не просто експериментують з AI, а інтегрують його в основу бізнес-стратегії, операцій і управлінських рішень. Для SAP це визнання того, що штучний інтелект уже став частиною цифрової архітектури рішень і допомагає компаніям підвищувати ефективність, автоматизувати процеси та створювати нові цінні сервіси.

Центральним елементом AI-екосистеми SAP є генеративний асистент Joule, інтегрований у хмарні рішення компанії. Він допомагає працювати з бізнес-даними в контексті, надає релевантні рекомендації, прискорює ухвалення рішень та відкриває нові сценарії автоматизації для фінансів, логістики, HR та інших напрямів.

SAP також розвиває власні AI-моделі, орієнтовані на роботу з бізнес-даними — від прогнозування ключових показників до аналізу великих масивів інформації й прискорення розробки корпоративних цифрових рішень. Усе це формує концепцію SAP Business AI, яка дозволяє вбудовувати інтелектуальні можливості безпосередньо в щоденні процеси компаній.

Чи плануєте ви вже цього року запуск або масштабування AI-проєктів на базі рішень SAP у своїй компанії?

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Gurram Dhanunjay Reddy and I am passionate about learning SAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! I'm Gurram Dhanunjaya Reddy, and I'm a student studying enterprise technologies and SAP. I'm curious about how companies handle production, supply chains, and business operations using programs like SAP.

I have been investigating various SAP modules and ideas throughout my studies, including enterprise resource planning systems, business process management, and production planning. I like discovering how technology can boost corporate productivity and address actual business issues.

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative.

What is your professional life dream?

My career goal is to work as a proficient SAP consultant or technology specialist who can assist businesses in streamlining their operations. I want to assist businesses in their digital transformation by working with cutting-edge enterprise systems. Being a part of that change would mean a lot to me because I think SAP technologies are crucial to modern businesses.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

I envision myself working in the technology or enterprise software sector and developing a solid grasp of SAP systems within the next five years. I want to work on practical projects where I can put my technical expertise to use and keep learning about new technologies like artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and data analytics. My objective is to develop my ability to use technology to solve challenging business issues.

What is your hobby?

Developing my technical skills and learning about new technologies are two of my favourite pastimes. I like experimenting with business systems, programming, and problem-solving exercises. I occasionally enjoy reading about technological advancements and how they are influencing various industries.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

The importance of perseverance and hard work is among the most significant lessons my parents instilled in me. They consistently inspired me to never give up in the face of difficulties and to never stop learning. This way of thinking keeps me motivated for both my academic work and personal growth.

What do you imagine is the future of technology topics like AI and Cloud ?

Technologies like cloud computing and artificial intelligence, in my opinion, will be crucial to businesses in the future. A lot of businesses are already shifting their systems to the cloud and utilising AI for data analysis and better decision-making. Businesses will become more automated, data-driven, and efficient in the future thanks to these technologies. Professionals who are familiar with these technologies will, in my opinion, have a lot of opportunities.

My Experience with SAP TechEd Bangalore

 

I had the chance to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore 2026 this year, and it was a really worthwhile and thrilling experience for me. SAP experts, developers, students, and tech professionals from various locations came together for the event to exchange ideas and knowledge about the most recent advancements in SAP.

I had the opportunity to attend a number of sessions and presentations during the event where specialists discussed emerging trends and technologies within the SAP ecosystem. Many sessions focused on topics such as SAP S/4HANA, cloud technologies, artificial intelligence, and digital transformation. Seeing how these technologies are assisting businesses in streamlining their operations and increasing productivity was fascinating.

 

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

Feel free to share your answer in the comments and tell us about your goals and dreams.

entry.title: 🗓️ Save the date SAP Inside Track Sydney 2026 — Reimagined!
entry.content:
Save the date for SAP Inside Track Sydney — Reimagined!
A new venue, an elevated experience, and a sharper agenda — with the same community spirit.

🗓️ Date: Friday, September 4, 2026
📍Location: Sydney CBD (stay tuned!)

More details coming soon, including registration, call for speakers, and sponsorship opportunities.

Subscribe to the Sydney Group for updates.

#SITSydney #SAPCommunity #SAPInsideTrack #Sydney

entry.title: Керівну директорку SAP Ukraine номіновано до рейтингу «ТОП-50 лідерів українського бізнесу»
entry.content:
Друзі, маємо чудову новину для нашої SAP-спільноти!✨

Ганну Мікулицьку, керівну директорку SAP Ukraine, номіновано до рейтингу «ТОП-50 лідерів українського бізнесу» 2026 року 🙌
На сайті delo.ua триває голосування за найкращих керівників українських компаній. За його результатами експертне журі та редакція визначать тих, хто створює нову економіку країни.

У своїй роботі Ганна керується принципами, які сьогодні формують нову якість лідерства:
Прозорість і відповідальність, сміливість, а також людяність.
Як лідер, вона системно розвиває SAP-екосистему в Україні: сприяє цифровій трансформації бізнесу, розвиває партнерську мережу, активно підтримує державні ініціативи, спрямовані на розвиток IT-індустрії та зміцнення економіки країни.

Особливу увагу вона приділяє зростанню й розвитку локальної команди, створенню мотивуючого середовища, а також забезпеченню безпеки, добробуту та турботи про здоров’я співробітників.

Ганна не лише допомагає у трансформації бізнесу, а й підсилює голос України на глобальній арені.
Підтримайте Ганну своїм голосом💙💛
Голосування триває до 20 числа включно.

Проголосувати можна тут:
https://delo.ua/business/startuvalo-golosuvannya-reitingu-top-50-lideriv-ukrayinskogo-biznesu-2026-…

entry.title: SIT Joinville 2026: que dia incrível vivemos juntos! 🎉
entry.content:
O SIT Joinville 2026 foi um sucesso do início ao fim! 🎉

No último sábado, vivenciamos um dia de intensa troca de conhecimento, networking e forte engajamento da comunidade SAP. A presença e participação ativa de todos reforçam o crescimento e a relevância do ecossistema na região.

Ver o evento com casa cheia e um público tão conectado mostrou que estamos no caminho certo, construindo um ambiente cada vez mais colaborativo e de geração de valor 💙

Nosso agradecimento a todos que fizeram parte deste momento: palestrantes, curadores, voluntários e participantes, que contribuíram para tornar o evento verdadeiramente especial.

Um reconhecimento especial aos nossos patrocinadores oficiais, que tornaram este encontro possível:

CVA Consultoria
CHpro Solutions
Escotta Consulting
MakeValue
Stefanini Brasil
Áxe Tecnologia
COINOV

Seguimos fortalecendo a comunidade SAP Joinville e ampliando conexões que impulsionam conhecimento e inovação.
Até a próxima edição! 🚀

 ##SAPInsideTrack #SITJoinville #SAPCommunit

Curadores: 
@GislaineFA  @HannahFavarin  @lucasabap  @LeonardoAF1 @Juliane_Schmitzhaus @MRobalinho  @RobertaFernande

entry.title: Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Elisângela Martins
entry.content:
Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Elisângela Martins

Esta publicação faz parte da série Somos SAP Community Curitiba.
A ideia da série está neste post de referência: 🎙️ Série da comunidade: Somos SAP Community Curit... - SAP Community

Fui convidado(a) por Simone Azevedo para participar da série
Somos SAP Community Curitiba.

Você pode conferir o post dela: About SimoneAzevedo - SAP Community

👩‍⚕️Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?

Sou Elisângela Martins, consultora SAP FI. Venho utilizando a comunidade no meu dia a dia para buscar soluções técnicas, além de aprendizagem, capacitação e acompanhamento das inovações.

 O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?
O que mais me motiva é o fato das pessoas trazerem sempre novidades e compartilharem seus conhecimentos, pois a comunidade é um grande repositório de respostas para dúvidas sobre todos os módulos.

Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

Após um período difícil de pandemia, gestação e mudança de Estado (Rio de Janeiro-RJ para Navegantes-SC), sem rede de apoio, acabei ficando desalocada. Realizei diversas buscas e participações em entrevistas sem sucesso, já desanimada, surgiu a oportunidade de participar do evento do SAP INSIDE TRACK Curitiba 2024!

Fiquei muito feliz, pois vislumbrei uma ótima oportunidade de me reconectar com as pessoas da área, me atualizar com as novas tecnologias e tendências do mercado SAP.

Chegando no evento me surpreendi positivamente com a organização, atenção dos curadores e o alto nível de conhecimento dos palestrantes trazendo assuntos importantes para nossa área. E isto, fez com que eu me sentisse VIVA, PERTENCENTE e MOTIVADA!🏆💪🌱

Esta foto, representa muito para mim, eu fiquei muito emocionada nesse dia, pois a minha família me acompanhou, acolheu e me deu muita força para seguir em frente!❤️🙏

Que conselho você daria para quem está começando agora no ecossistema SAP e ainda não conhece a comunidade?

A SAP Community é vital para nós consultores, desenvolvedores e parceiros SAP, onde nos conectamos com pessoas para compartilhar conhecimentos, resolver problemas técnicos, além de poder acompanhar as inovações da SAP em tempo real.

     Conecte-se comigo

LinkedIn 👉 www.linkedin.com/in/elisângela-martins-18b42321

Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido:

@Natacha_Wistuba e @MarcosFerreira 

a compartilharem suas histórias na comunidade.

Além das perguntas da série:

 Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?

 O que mais me motiva a continuar fazendo parte da SAP Community Curitiba?

 Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

E deixo também uma pergunta adicional:

O que vocês gostariam de ter sabido antes de participar do primeiro evento da SAP Community?

 Frase final

“Cada experiência compartilhada é um passo a mais na construção de uma comunidade mais forte e colaborativa.”

#SomosSAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunityCWB
#SAPCommunity

entry.title: ✨ SAP Talk Women CWB 2026: um encontro que se sente, se vive e se leva pra vida
entry.content:
A segunda edição do SAP Talk Women CWB foi daquelas experiências que a gente não explica… a gente sente. 


Realizado na Hotmilk, por meio da parceria com a PUCPR, vivemos uma noite especial no espaço Innosphera — um ambiente que respira tecnologia, inovação e, acima de tudo, conexão.


Desde a chegada, o cuidado já era visível: nossa voluntária @TalisMoraes  recebeu cada participante com um sorriso no rosto, entregando uma rosa e um cartão de boas-vindas .

Um gesto simples, mas cheio de significado — porque cada mulher ali importa.
A noite começou com uma abertura acolhedora, onde compartilhei a história do evento — que nasceu com um propósito muito claro: conectar, inspirar e fortalecer mulheres no ecossistema SAP e tecnologia.


E foi exatamente isso que vivemos.
A dinâmica de pintura de taças trouxe leveza, criatividade e conexão.
Cada participante recebeu uma taça como um brinde especial do evento — e ali, entre pincéis, cores e conversas, vimos novas conexões surgirem e momentos genuínos de troca acontecerem de forma leve e espontânea.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Tivemos a honra de receber @antonia_zorn , da SAP Community Global, que nos trouxe uma mensagem poderosa sobre comunidade.
Ela falou sobre coragem — de pedir ajuda, de ajudar e de se colocar.
E, com ainda mais significado, realizou uma de suas primeiras palestras em português, nos inspirando pela sua autenticidade.


Na sequência, @sil_fumura2026 nos conduziu a uma reflexão profunda.
Com dados e histórias, mostrou o quanto o universo da tecnologia é vasto e cheio de oportunidades — e o quanto nós, mulheres, ainda temos espaço para crescer e ocupar.
Reforçou que acreditar no nosso potencial não é opcional — é necessário.
E que, juntas, ampliamos nossas vozes, nossos espaços e nossos resultados.

 

 

Nossos patrocinadores também compartilharam suas histórias e reforçaram seu compromisso com o fortalecimento das mulheres na tecnologia — caminhando lado a lado com esse movimento.

 

CHPRO | CVA | LUPUS | SMARTONE

Ao longo do evento, experiências especiais tornaram a noite ainda mais completa:
ADCOS Dermocosméticos, proporcionando momentos de cuidado e conexão com o autocuidado
Aesthi, com quick massage, trazendo pausas de relaxamento em meio à energia do evento
Morana, com a exposição de semijoias, trazendo mais uma opção de experiência para quem quisesse aproveitar um momento de leveza, estilo e expressão pessoal


Encerramos com o sorteio de brindes, oferecidos com carinho por empresas parceiras

e finalizamos com a nossa foto oficial — com sorrisos, brilho nos olhos e a certeza de que vivemos algo especial 

 

 

 

 

 

 

A segunda edição foi um sucesso.
E ela só aconteceu graças às nossas patrocinadoras, apoiadores e todas as pessoas que acreditam nesse propósito.


Um agradecimento especial também às voluntárias, que estiveram presentes em cada detalhe, acolhendo, organizando e cuidando de tudo com muito carinho — vocês fizeram toda a diferença 

Talis, Priscila, Simone, Natacha e Beatriz

Mas, principalmente, graças a cada mulher que esteve presente.
Vocês são o coração desse evento.
Obrigada por se permitirem estar, trocar, se abrir, aprender e inspirar.
É essa energia que transforma um evento em um movimento.

 E se você não esteve conosco este ano… fica aqui o convite:

em março de 2027, esperamos você para viver tudo isso com a gente. 

Conecte-se com a SAP Community Curitiba
Quer acompanhar as novidades da comunidade ou participar das próximas iniciativas?
Siga nossos canais:

LinkedIn

Instagram

Grupo WhatsApp da comunidade

entry.title: Hello SAP Community My Name is Manasa
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community! 

My name is Gamidi.Manasa and I’m currently learning SAP with a focus on Sales and Distribution (SD). I have a strong interest in understanding end-to-end business processes like Order-to-Cash (O2C), pricing, ATP, and billing. I enjoy exploring how different SAP modules like SD, MM, and FICO integrate to support real business scenarios.

I am passionate about learning new concepts in SAP and applying them through projects and presentations. Recently, I worked on understanding ATP (Available-to-Promise), requirement types, and schedule line behavior, which helped me gain deeper functional knowledge.

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a skilled SAP consultant who can design and deliver effective business solutions. I aspire to work on real-time implementation projects where I can understand client requirements, configure the system, and provide end-to-end solutions. I also want to continuously upgrade my knowledge in SAP, especially in areas like SAP SD, S/4HANA, and business process integration, so that I can add value to organizations and grow as a domain expert.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next five years, I see myself as an experienced SAP SD consultant working on complex business scenarios and handling client interactions confidently. I aim to gain strong expertise in core areas like Order-to-Cash (O2C), pricing, billing, and integration with other modules like MM and FICO. I also see myself mentoring beginners and sharing knowledge within the SAP community.

What is your hobby?

My hobby is continuously learning and exploring new concepts, especially in SAP and technology. I enjoy spending time understanding real-time business scenarios, practicing configurations, and improving my knowledge through hands-on learning. Apart from that, I also like enhancing my communication and presentation skills, which helps me express my ideas more clearly. These activities not only keep me engaged but also support my personal and professional growth.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

The best lesson my parents taught me is the importance of consistency, patience, and continuous learning. They always encouraged me to stay focused on my goals and not give up when things become challenging. This mindset helps me stay motivated in my SAP learning journey and keeps me moving forward step by step.

What do you predict is the future of SAP?

I believe the future of SAP is strongly moving towards cloud-based solutions, automation, and artificial intelligence. With platforms like SAP S/4HANA and SAP BTP, businesses are becoming more intelligent and data-driven. In the coming years, SAP will play a major role in digital transformation by integrating AI, real-time analytics, and automation into business processes, making organizations more efficient and agile.

My Experience with SAP TechEd Bangalore

Attending SAP TechEd Bangalore 2025 this year was a special and memorable experience for me. It was not just an event, but a great opportunity to see how the SAP world connects people, ideas, and technology.

I got to learn about new developments in SAP like S/4HANA, cloud, and AI in a very practical way. The sessions were interesting and helped me understand how these technologies are used in real business scenarios.One thing I really enjoyed was interacting with different people—experts, professionals, and fellow learners. It made me feel more confident and motivated in my learning journey.

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

What is your professional life dream?

I would love to hear your thoughts in the comments

entry.title: The Joule Effect: Event Recap ⚡️
entry.content:
Last week at the SAP Casablanca office, we brought together SAP professionals and enthusiasts for our event The Joule Effect, a focused session on the future of Business AI.

With the support of the SAP Community team, and contributions from @Salah2  and @belahmew, we explored what SAP Joule really means for our ecosystem.

Key Takeaways

  • AI is changing the game: Faster, simpler SAP interactions
  • Clean Core is essential: The foundation for scalable and secure AI adoption
  • Consultants remain key: Technology evolves, but expertise drives real transformation

Beyond the Content

What stood out most was the exchange between experienced professionals and emerging SAP talents. This is exactly what we want to build in Casablanca: a space to learn, connect, and grow together.

What’s Next

This is just the start. Our goal is to establish a consistent SAP knowledge hub in Morocco.

If you missed it:

  • Follow the Casablanca tag on SAP Community
  • Connect with us on LinkedIn
  • Share what topic we should cover next

Thank you to everyone who made this first step a success. More is coming.

entry.title: Wollongong Stammtisch Recap Mar '26
entry.content:
The Wollongong Stammtisch has earned its place in the calendar with another fun meetup.  Once again we had a roughly even split of locals and Sydneysiders.  We were excited to meet Pranoti and Nalla for whom this was their first Stammtisch. We hope to see them at the next Sydney event, which will likely be in May

John & OnurThirroul BeachPaul, Mark, Pranoti & NallaSimon and MeGraham & SimonGlen & Scott

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Lakshmi Triveni and I am excited to start my journey.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

My name is Sandireddy Lakshmi Triveni, and I’m a final-year Computer Science student at Chalapathi Institute of Engineering and Technology.I am a SAP Certified Associate with hands-on experience in SAP MM configuration, procure-to-pay cycle execution, and integration with FI and SD modules. Alongside my technical journey, I’ve also achieved a 357-day Duolingo streak, which reflects my persistence and commitment to daily learning.

As part of the Hello SAP Community Initiative, I’m excited to share my experience. You can read more about the initiative in Hello SAP Community Initiative , which gives the full background and context.

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a skilled SAP consultant who can bridge the gap between business requirements and technical solutions. I want to empower organizations by simplifying complex SAP processes into clear, practical workflows that anyone can understand.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In five years, I see myself leading SAP MM and S/4HANA implementation projects, mentoring juniors, and contributing actively to the SAP Community through blogs, tutorials, and presentations. I also envision myself expanding into AI-driven SAP solutions, combining my certification in Generative AI with enterprise applications.

What is your hobby?

My favorite hobby is reading books. I love immersing myself in different perspectives and stories, which not only relaxes me but also inspires creativity and fresh ideas in my professional work. Reading helps me stay curious and continuously expand my knowledge.

Share a fun fact/story about yourself that people don’t know

A fun fact about me is that I’m very disciplined when it comes to learning. For example, I’ve maintained a 357-day Duolingo streak without missing a single day. It started as a small challenge, but now it’s a symbol of persistence and consistency in my life qualities I also bring into my SAP journey.

What was the most fun project you ever participated in and why?

The most fun project I worked on was configuring the SAP MM pricing procedure. At first, the steps and counters felt confusing, but once I understood how condition types and schema assignments worked, everything clicked into place. I enjoyed troubleshooting errors and documenting each step in a clear, beginner-friendly way. This project gave me confidence and reinforced my passion for simplifying SAP concepts for learners.

 

My SAP TechEd Bangalore Experience

This year I had the opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore 2026, and it was truly inspiring. Being surrounded by professionals, students, and experts from across the SAP ecosystem gave me a sense of belonging to a global community. I attended sessions on SAP S/4HANA innovations, AI integration, and sustainability in enterprise systems, which broadened my perspective on how technology is shaping the future of businesses.

One of the highlights was interacting with mentors and peers who shared practical insights into SAP MM and its integration with other modules. The hands-on workshops helped me connect theory with real-world applications, and I left with renewed motivation to keep learning and contributing.

And now let’s hear from you!

Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life? Share your thoughts in the comments . I’d love to read them!

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Purnima and I'm Passionate About Building My Career in SAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

I’m Purnima, a B.Tech graduate from Chalapathi Institute of Technology, currently working on an SAP FICO implementation project.

 

Through this experience, I am developing strong knowledge in Financial Accounting (FI) and Controlling (CO), working with areas such as General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, and Cost Center Accounting. I am actively involved in configuration, testing, and business process analysis.

I enjoy understanding how SAP supports financial operations, enhances cost control, and enables data-driven decision-making. I am excited to further build my expertise in SAP FICO.

 

Here are the questions I’ve chosen to answer as a part of the "Hello SAP Community" initiative.

What is your professional life dream?

My professional dream is to become a skilled SAP FICO consultant who can help organizations manage their financial processes efficiently. I aim to contribute to business growth by providing accurate financial insights and improving decision-making through SAP solutions.

Where do you see yourself in 5 years?

In the next 5 years, I see myself as an experienced SAP FICO consultant with strong expertise in implementation and support projects. I aim to work on real-time business scenarios, handle complex requirements, and continuously upgrade my skills in SAP and related technologies.

What is your hobby?

My hobbies include learning new technologies, improving my communication skills, and exploring SAP-related concepts. I also enjoy spending time enhancing my knowledge, which helps me grow both personally and professionally.

What is the best lesson your parents taught you?

The best lesson my parents taught me is the importance of hard work, discipline, and consistency. They always encouraged me to stay focused on my goals, be patient during challenges, and never give up. This mindset helps me stay motivated and continuously improve in my career.

Which 5 things do you absolutely want to achieve in life?

  1. To become a successful SAP FICO consultant with strong expertise in financial processes
  2. To continuously learn and upgrade my skills in SAP and new technologies
  3. To achieve financial independence and stability
  4. To contribute to organizational success through my knowledge and work
  5. To maintain a good balance between my personal and professional life.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore Experience

This year, I had the incredible opportunity to attend SAP TechEd Bangalore 2026, and it was truly a rewarding experience. The event brought together developers, architects, and technology enthusiasts from across the ecosystem, creating a vibrant space for learning and collaboration.


From insightful keynote sessions to hands-on workshops, every moment was packed with value. I particularly enjoyed exploring the latest innovations in AI, cloud technologies, and SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). The live demos and real-world use cases helped bridge the gap between theory and practical application.

One of the most valuable aspects was networking—connecting with like-minded professionals, SAP experts, and industry leaders. These conversations opened new perspectives and ideas that I’m excited to take forward in my work.

 

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

What is your personal life dream?

Feel free to share your answer in the comments and tell us about your goals and dreams.

entry.title: Hello SAP Community! My name is Kavuri Revathi and I'm excited to begin my journey in the SAP world.
entry.content:
My name is Kavuri Revathi and I'm student at Chalapathi Institute of Engineering And Technology in Guntur, Andhra Pradesh, India.

I am currently pursuing my B.Tech final year in Computer Science and Engineering. Along with my academic studies, I have completed my certification in SAP End-to-End Business Process. This helped me understand how different business functions like procurement, inventory management, and finance are integrated within SAP systems.

What is your professional life dream?

My career goal is to become a skilled SAP consultant who can help organizations improve their business processes and efficiency. I want to work on real-time projects and contribute to digital transformation by using SAP solutions.

What is your Hobby?


My hobbies include travelling, learning new technologies and improving my technical skills . I enjoy exploring SAP concepts, practicing different business scenarios, and enhancing my problem-solving abilities. I also like reading about emerging technologies and how they impact industries.

What is the best lesson you have learned so far?

One of the most important lessons I have learned is the value of continuous learning and consistency. In the field of technology, staying updated and practicing regularly is key to success. This mindset helps me stay motivated and focused on my goals.

My SAP TechEd Bangalore experience

Recently, I had the opportunity to attend the SAP TechEd event in Bangalore, and it was truly an incredible experience for me. It was inspiring to meet SAP experts and senior professionals and see how humble and approachable they were while interacting with participants.

I also got the chance to connect with people from different organizations, which helped me understand the wide range of opportunities available in the SAP ecosystem. Through these interactions, I learned how companies are adopting new technologies like Artificial Intelligence and Cloud to transform their business processes.

During the event, I also appeared for the Generative AI certification exam at the center. Even though there were a few technical issues, the support team handled the situation very calmly and professionally, which made the experience smooth and comfortable.

The sessions and speaker presentations were highly insightful. Overall, this experience motivated me to continue learning and building my career in SAP and emerging technologies.

And now let’s hear from you!

I would love to hear your thoughts.

What is the best lesson you have learned?

Feel free to share your answer in the comments and tell us about your goals and dreams.

entry.title: SAP Community Hobbies Series : [Not Just a Hobby: A Story of Small Classrooms and Big Dreams]
entry.content:
Hi, My name is Abhijna,  I am  an engineering student and I would like to share about my journey of teaching and connecting with young minds as part of the SAP Community Hobbies series.  I am a student with a deep curiosity for connections in all forms. Whether it’s understanding how data travels through networks or building meaningful connections with people, I find myself drawn to both worlds equally. With a strong interest in electronics and networking, I enjoy exploring how systems work, and at the same time, how small human interactions can create lasting impact. And somewhere between these two, I found a journey that’s just getting started.
Coming from a family where teaching has always been more than just a profession, I grew up witnessing the quiet impact it can create. Among all those influences, my grandfather has been my biggest inspiration. Not because of titles or achievements, but because of the way people still remember him, with respect, gratitude, and stories of how he shaped their lives..

About a year or two ago, I visited a  school, one where my grandfather had once taught. I didn’t go there with a plan. It was just a visit. But what I experienced there stayed with me. The respect people had for him, the way they remembered him, and the number of lives he had impacted, it made me pause. It made me think. What if I could become even a small reason for someone to move forward in life?

That thought stayed. And eventually, it turned into action. I went back not as a visitor this time, but as someone who wanted to give. I started small. I spent a week teaching dance, just to connect. And then I began talking to students about things they usually don’t get exposed to, what engineering is, what opportunities exist, what lies beyond what they see every day.

And that’s when I truly understood something. There is a different kind of energy in  school classrooms. The students there don’t just sit and listen , they want to learn. There is curiosity, there is respect, and there is this quiet hunger for knowledge. They are waiting for someone to show them what’s possible.

And being able to stand there, even for a short time, felt like a responsibility , and also a privilege. Of course, it wasn’t always easy.

Standing in front of students who are just a few years younger than you makes you question yourself. Will they understand? Will this make sense to them? Especially when you’re trying to explain concepts like electronics or embedded systems, things that even we find complex. But over time, I realized something simple. If you can relate knowledge to life, anyone can understand it. When I started breaking things down into examples they could see and feel , like explaining sensors through real-life situations , their response changed completely. It wasn’t confusion anymore. It was curiosity. One moment, in particular, stays with me. At the end of a session, a 7th-grade student came up to me and confidently named two sensors we had discussed. Just two. But that moment meant everything. Because it showed me that something had stayed with him. And sometimes, that’s all it takes.

A small understanding. A small spark. A small beginning.

There are many misconceptions around  schools, that it’s difficult to engage, that students may not be interested, or that opportunities to contribute are limited. But my experience has been the opposite. When approached in the right way, schools are open. And the students? They are ready. All they need is someone to show up.

When I think about how this connects to the SAP world, I see a strong parallel. SAP is all about creating impact through structured systems, data, and processes. Similarly, what I do involves identifying gaps, organizing knowledge, and delivering it in a way that creates meaningful outcomes. It’s about enabling growth, improving accessibility, and making information useful ,just like SAP does, but in a human, grassroots way.

This journey has also changed the way I look at learning. To teach even a little, I’ve realized I need to keep learning constantly. It’s not a one-way process. It’s a cycle , where giving and learning go hand in hand. And somewhere along the way, this stopped feeling like just a hobby. It became something more personal. Something that connects who I am, where I come from, and what I want to become. Because at the end of the day, it’s not always about doing something big. Sometimes, it’s just about showing up in a small classroom , and becoming a reason for someone to dream bigger.
